task_completion.description: Open Jurism Preferences and navigate to the Export tab
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism Preferences window is open and the 'Export' tab (or Quick Copy settings area) is selected.

task_completion.description: Configure Quick Copy format to JM Indigo or Bluebook
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Default Format dropdown in the Quick Copy settings is set to 'JM Indigo (Law Review)' or 'Bluebook Law Review'.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Brown v. Board of Education' case in Jurism
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Jurism window shows the case 'Brown v. Board of Education' selected in the item list.

task_completion.description: Open the target text file to save the citation
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, mousepad) is open, and the window title or file path indicates it is editing '/home/ga/Documents/brown_citation.txt'.

task_completion.description: Paste the correctly formatted citation into the text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains the citation in Bluebook format, which must show the case name first, then the reporter (347 U.S. 483), and end with the year (1954). Example: 'Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954)'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Jurism GUI to configure settings and copy the citation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Jurism application open, settings being changed, and the item being selected/copied, rather than the agent just guessing the citation.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'echo "Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954)" > /home/ga/Documents/brown_citation.txt'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Quick Copy feature rather than manually typing the citation from memory
integrity.visual_evidence: The citation in the text editor should appear pasted (e.g., appearing all at once between frames) and exactly match standard Jurism output, rather than being typed out character-by-character.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target case is 'Brown v. Board of Education'. The official citation details are: Reporter '347 U.S. 483', Year '1954'. The correct Bluebook/Indigo citation format must have the case name first, followed by the reporter, and ending with the year in parentheses (e.g., 'Brown v. Board of Education, 347 U.S. 483 (1954)'). The output must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/brown_citation.txt'.
